title: Too Old for the Piano?
summary: A 70-year-old man in Seoul and his young teacher disagree about why he should learn music.

body.speaker: Mr. Han
body.text: I am seventy years old. I want to learn the piano. But maybe I am too old. My fingers are slow.

body.speaker: Jisoo
body.text: You are not too old, Mr. Han. But I must be honest. You will never play like a young student. That part is true.

body.speaker: Mr. Han
body.text: So why try? When I was young, I wanted to be the best. If I cannot be good, what is the point?

body.speaker: Jisoo
body.text: You think music is about being the best. For a child, maybe. The child wants prizes. But you do not need a prize.

body.speaker: Mr. Han
body.text: Then what do I need? I have only a few years left. I should not waste them.

body.speaker: Jisoo
body.text: You are not wasting them. A young student plays for the future. You play for right now. That is a gift, not a loss.

body.speaker: Mr. Han
body.text: Right now... I never thought of it like that. When I work, I always think of tomorrow.

body.speaker: Jisoo
body.text: Yes. But the piano does not care about tomorrow. When you play one note, you only have that note. Nothing else.

body.speaker: Mr. Han
body.text: My grandson plays fast and well. He is bored. He looks at the clock.

body.speaker: Jisoo
body.text: And you are slow, but you smile. So tell me, Mr. Han. Who really has the music?

body.speaker: Mr. Han
body.text: Maybe the slow man. Maybe I am not too late. Maybe I am just in time.

annotations.meaningVi: một món quà, không phải một mất mát

grammarNotes.title: too + adjective
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'too + tính từ' nghĩa là quá mức cần thiết.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: "too" before an adjective means more than needed, as in "maybe I am too old."

grammarNotes.title: First conditional with if
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'if + hiện tại đơn, + tương lai/can' để nói điều kiện.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use if for conditions, as in "If I cannot be good, what is the point?"

grammarNotes.title: should / should not for advice
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'should' và 'should not' đưa ra lời khuyên.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: "should" gives advice, as in "I should not waste them."

translationVi:
Mr. Han: Tôi đã bảy mươi tuổi. Tôi muốn học đàn piano. Nhưng có lẽ tôi quá già rồi. Ngón tay tôi chậm chạp.
Jisoo: Bác không quá già đâu, bác Han. Nhưng cháu phải nói thật. Bác sẽ không bao giờ chơi được như một học trò trẻ. Điều đó là sự thật.
Mr. Han: Vậy thì cố gắng làm gì? Hồi trẻ, tôi muốn trở thành người giỏi nhất. Nếu không giỏi được thì có ý nghĩa gì đâu?
Jisoo: Bác nghĩ âm nhạc là để trở thành người giỏi nhất. Với một đứa trẻ thì có lẽ vậy. Đứa trẻ muốn giải thưởng. Nhưng bác đâu cần giải thưởng.
Mr. Han: Vậy tôi cần gì? Tôi chỉ còn vài năm nữa thôi. Tôi không nên lãng phí chúng.
Jisoo: Bác không lãng phí đâu. Một học trò trẻ chơi đàn cho tương lai. Bác chơi cho hiện tại. Đó là một món quà, không phải một mất mát.
Mr. Han: Hiện tại... Tôi chưa bao giờ nghĩ theo cách đó. Khi làm việc, tôi luôn nghĩ về ngày mai.
Jisoo: Vâng. Nhưng cây đàn piano không bận tâm về ngày mai. Khi bác chơi một nốt nhạc, bác chỉ có nốt nhạc đó thôi. Không gì khác.
Mr. Han: Cháu trai tôi chơi nhanh và giỏi. Nó thấy chán. Nó cứ nhìn đồng hồ.
Jisoo: Còn bác thì chậm, nhưng bác mỉm cười. Vậy bác nói cháu nghe, bác Han. Ai mới thật sự có âm nhạc?
Mr. Han: Có lẽ là người chậm. Có lẽ tôi không quá muộn. Có lẽ tôi vừa kịp lúc.

questions.prompt: What does Mr. Han want to learn?

options: He will play like a young student
He will never play like a young student
questions.explanation: "You will never play like a young student. That part is true."

questions.prompt: What did Mr. Han want when he was young?
questions.explanation: "When I was young, I wanted to be the best."

questions.prompt: According to Jisoo, what does a child play for?
questions.explanation: "The child wants prizes" and "A young student plays for the future."

questions.prompt: What does Jisoo say Mr. Han plays for?
questions.explanation: "You play for right now. That is a gift, not a loss."

questions.prompt: What does "waste" mean in the passage?
options: To use time badly with no good result
questions.explanation: "I should not waste them" — use his few years badly.
questions.hint: Mr. Han worries about his years.

questions.prompt: How does Mr. Han usually think when he works?
questions.explanation: "When I work, I always think of tomorrow."

questions.prompt: How does Mr. Han's grandson act at the piano?
options: He is bored and looks at the clock
questions.explanation: "He is bored. He looks at the clock."
questions.hint: Mr. Han talks about his grandson.

questions.prompt: What does Mr. Han decide at the end?
questions.explanation: "Maybe I am not too late. Maybe I am just in time."

questions.prompt: What true thing does Jisoo's view and Mr. Han's view share?
options: That skill and joy are different things
That only the young should play
questions.explanation: Jisoo admits he won't be skilled, yet shows joy is the real gift — both accept skill is limited.
questions.hint: Both speakers accept one hard fact about his playing.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: Old people should not try new things
Joy in the moment can matter more than being the best
Piano is only for the young
questions.explanation: "You play for right now. That is a gift, not a loss."

blanks.explanation: "A young student plays for the future."
"That is a gift, not a loss."
"He is bored. He looks at the clock."

criticalThinkingQuestion: Jisoo says the old man playing slowly may have 'the music' more than the bored, skilled grandson. Can someone who is worse at a skill still get more from it than an expert?
